topic.name: Administrative Law in Singapore
topic.description: This cluster discusses various aspects of administrative law in Singapore, including the doctrine of legitimate expectation, threshold issues for judicial review, and the exclusion of judicial review.

Document 1: The doctrine of legitimate expectation in Singapore protects both procedural and substantive rights.
The recognition of substantive legitimate expectations is somewhat controversial.
Document 2: Threshold issues are legal requirements in Singapore administrative law that must be satisfied by applicants before their claims for judicial review.
Applicants must establish they have private standing if they seek to represent personal interests.
Document 3: Exclusion of judicial review has been attempted by the Parliament of Singapore to protect the exercise of executive power.
The courts there have taken the view that such clauses are, subject to some exceptions, not effective in denying or restricting the extent to which the courts are able to exercise judicial review.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the annual rowing competition between the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge.

Document 1: The Boat Race is an annual side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course on the River Thames in southwest London.
Document 2: The Boat Race is an annual side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities, as of 2014 it is followed throughout the United Kingdom and broadcast worldwide.
Document 3: Held annually, the Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.

topic.name: Evolutionary Adaptations in Organisms
topic.description: This topic encompasses the various evolutionary adaptations and strategies organisms employ for survival and reproduction in response to environmental pressures.

Document 1: Worker policing is a behavior seen in colonies of social hymenopterans whereby worker females eat or remove eggs that have been laid by other workers rather than those laid by a queen.
Worker policing has been suggested as a form of coercion to promote the evolution of altruistic behavior in eusocial insect societies.
Document 2: Plant defense against herbivory or host-plant resistance describes a range of adaptations evolved by plants which improve their survival and reproduction by reducing the impact of herbivores.
Plants can sense being touched, and they can use several strategies to defend against damage caused by herbivores.
Document 3: Entoprocta is a phylum of mostly sessile aquatic animals, ranging from 0.1 to 7 millimetres long.
Some species eject unfertilized ova into the water, while others keep their ova in brood chambers until they hatch.

topic.name: Diving Decompression Practices
topic.description: The documents discuss the processes, equipment, and safety protocols related to decompression in diving, focusing on the management of inert gases and the ascent procedures required to prevent decompression sickness.

Document 1: The practice of decompression by divers comprises the planning and monitoring of the profile indicated by the algorithms or tables of the chosen decompression model
Omission of decompression theoretically required for a dive profile exposes the diver to significantly higher risk of symptomatic decompression sickness.
Document 2: A diving cylinder, scuba tank or diving tank is a gas cylinder used to store and transport the high pressure breathing gas required by a scuba set
Cylinders provide gas to the diver through the demand valve of a diving regulator or the breathing loop of a diving rebreather.
Document 3: The decompression of a diver is the reduction in ambient pressure experienced during ascent from depth
It is also the process of elimination of dissolved inert gases from the diver's body, which occurs during the ascent.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclone Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclone seasons and their characteristics in different oceanic regions.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Emilia was a rare tropical cyclone that affected the Baja California peninsula in July 2006.
The storm brought tropical storm force winds and precipitation to the southwestern Mexican coastline.
Document 2: The 1994 – 95 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was fairly active, with storms forming regularly from October through April.
Most of the storms originated from the intertropical convergence zone.
Document 3: The 1992 – 93 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season lasted longer than normal, with the first storm Aviona forming on September 27.
Tropical cyclones in this basin are monitored by the Regional Specialised Meteorological Centre in Réunion.

topic.name: Civic and Commercial Developments
topic.description: This topic encompasses various civic and commercial developments that serve community and retail functions in urban settings.

Document 1: Willow Grove Park Mall is a three-story shopping mall located in the unincorporated community of Willow Grove
The mall contains over 130 stores along with several restaurants including The Cheesecake Factory.
Document 2: The Oregon Civic Justice Center is a three-story former library building on the campus of Willamette University
the building now houses several programs of Willamette University College of Law.
Document 3: The Hillsboro Civic Center is a government-built, mixed-use development in downtown Hillsboro, Oregon
The complex includes retail space, public plazas, and residential housing.
